body_txt: Hermits are folks who don't live around other folks — and they like it that way. They aren't afraid of Dying Alone . Hermits aren't banished or cut off from society as punishment — they willingly chose to leave it, for whatever reason. Hermits can be either friendly or hostile. If a hermit is friendly, their reason for living alone will be that they feel more comfortable living alone . If the hermit is hostile, it's because they're misanthropic . It's also frequent for hermits to seek out solitude to concentrate on spiritual or intellectual contemplation; as such, religious hermits and solitary monks are a fairly common variant of this concept. Magic-users are also frequently depicted as hermitic sorts; the Wizard Classic , Witch Classic , and Solitary Sorceress are all often depicted as living in deep solitude, the former on top of a high, fortified spire from which they rarely descend and the latter two in secluded cottages deep in the woods, where they can study magic lore and cast their spells away from prying eyes. Nice or not, almost all hermits are quirky. That's because Loners Are Freaks ; they don't conform to society's rules about cleanliness , sociability , or footwear . Heck, they don't conform to society's standards at all. Hermits generally have a high tolerance to Go Mad from the Isolation , but they're not completely immune, as indicated by the mention of quirkiness. Most classic hermits tend to live in swamps, jungles, deserts, or caves, and are often very old. Modern hermits are more likely to live in cities or towns, but be very withdrawn, to the point of rarely socializing. The Hikikomori , Shrinking Violet , and Reclusive Artist are some examples of this sort. If a hero seeks out this person for guidance or training , chances are you're dealing with a Hermit Guru . Other times when a hero meets up with them on a journey, they may become a kind of one-person Wacky Wayside Tribe . Self-Imposed Exile is another common reason for becoming a hermit. Unlike other examples of The Exile , where exile is punishment, this hermit is choosing exile for themselves because they feel they must. Sometimes their continued freedom or survival depends on it . It may be a form of living Off the Grid . Use caution when adding examples from Eastern media — "hermit" is a popular translation for the Chinese xian and Japanese sennin, but not all xian/sennin embody this trope. Compare The Aloner , for whom social isolation is a terrible punishment, and those Sent Into Hiding . Contrast Hates Being Alone , for when a character really doesn't want to be isolated from others. See I Work Alone for when they don't rely on others.
